props.title: /dump /capture /sitrep — one substrate, three faces
props.subtitle: 5 parallel experts → synthesis → 3 negotiators → FINAL. 9 artifacts, 4 tensions, 15-step shipping sequence.

props.content: /capture writes. /sitrep reads. /dump is /capture at depth. Under all three: one trigger envelope, one substrate (filesystem), one projection (beads), one admission story. CDO synthesis turns 3 skills and 5 questions into a 15-step sequenced shipping plan with clear ownership between lifecycle-engine (write) and graph-cms (read).

props.label: THE TRIO
props.title: /dump · /capture · /sitrep
props.subtitle: Three skills, one substrate. The user's daily drivers.

props.title: 🧠 /dump
props.content:
**Deep mode** of /capture. Brain dump on everything — inventory + auto /research + /prior-art per topic + dispatch shovel-ready to /exec + /propose the rest.

**Nothing stays in memory.**

Calls `capture.sweep(mode=deep)` — runs the fidelity convergence loop with prior-art enrichment. Max 3 iterations without fidelity gain → halt + note gap.

Output: durable YAML in `.lev/pm/workstreams/<ws>/captures/YYYYMMDD-*.yaml`

props.title: 📥 /capture
props.content:
**Standard mode** inventory sweep. Classify → route → fidelity score → write.

**Order:** SAVE first, THEN show the delta.

Fidelity formula: 0.30·detail + 0.25·rel + 0.20·attr + 0.15·nbr + 0.10·action. Gate ≥ 0.8 to proceed to /propose.

Emits trigger: `audit://capture/<ws>/<ts>` (or `collapse://ws/<id>` under the emote model).

Calls `capture.sweep(mode=standalone)`.

props.title: 📊 /sitrep
props.content:
**Freestyle dashboard.** Reads the trace stream. Never writes.

Emits: `projection://sitrep/<ws>/<ts>` — a view primitive (read-only per dna/graph.yaml).

Projects 5 sections: exec brief · active entities · memory state · next actions · decisions needed. Inline emote dispatch (expand 2, collapse 4,5,6, admit 3).

**Theater check:** flags any /close trigger with `items != []` as ⚠.

Source: `.lev/pm/workstreams/*/trace/*.yaml` (NOT handoff archaeology).

props.label: PER-QUESTION VERDICTS
props.title: 5 T1 + 3 T3 deliberation outcomes
props.subtitle: Weighted by downstream blast radius. Q1 at 0.78 trusted above the 0.82 cluster (groupthink defector with direct code citations).

rows.claim: 5-stage chain fuses verb/envelope + extract/classify + classify/admission; reverses runtime arrow (LevEvent is INPUT)
Keep /capture as verb; /close Step 0 calls shared capture.sweep(mode=gate) — stops prose drift
Acts firing lifecycle_trigger.v1 stratified by binding × direction × depth (3-axis cube)
Safe IFF writer-side AJV validate + vocab check + sha256 receipt + collision detection
Overlays = schema contract (ship first); beads = storage backend (mirrors later)
Writer-side NOW, projection-side Sprint+1, kernel-side deferred (abac-policy-engine.ts is VAPORWARE)
~/.claude/skills/_shared/capture-sweep.md as single source; @include from both. Drift already real today.
Filesystem = substrate, bd = projection over it. No migration event — 'bd indexing turns on'.

props.label: CONVERGED ARCHITECTURE
props.title: Layers, owners, status
props.subtitle: Scope split between lifecycle-engine (write side) and graph-cms (read side) is clean

rows.artifacts: AJV validate + dna/emotes.yaml vocab check + sha256 + collision detection
promised by manifold header line 9; does NOT exist on disk
new --type=trigger bd kind; bd ready --epic --type=trigger = cursor

props.label: THE BIG FINDING
props.title: Admission layer — what was promised vs what exists
props.subtitle: T3-A vaporware discovery: dna/graph.yaml declares a gate the code does not have

props.title: ⚠ What dna/graph.yaml declares
props.content:
`dna/graph.yaml:12-33` declares the layering:

**Kernel layer**
- constraint-manifold (C1 Finitude, C2 Non-commutation)
- abac-policy-engine (C3 Nominalized, C4 Ratchet, C5 Locality)

**Projection layer**
- compositor (extract → classify → validate → apply)

Admission is supposed to happen kernel-side BEFORE projection. Every state mutation promised to pass through the admission enforcer.

C3-C5 `status: assumed`, implying "not yet proven, but real."

props.title: ✓ What the code actually has
props.content:
**`constraint-manifold.ts:18`:** `ConstraintId = 'F01_FINITUDE' | 'N01_NONCOMMUTATION'` — closed 2-item union.

**`constraint-manifold.ts:9`:** comment promises "C3-C5 governance assumptions live in abac-policy-engine.ts" — **file does not exist.**

**`grep constraint-manifold core/graph/`:** zero matches. Compositor does not call the kernel. The two "layers" are architecturally disjoint today.

**SchemaValidationHook:** 30 lines, checks `['id','type','source']` field presence on `upsert_entity`. That's the entire runtime gate.

C3-C5 are decorative.

props.label: 4 TENSIONS RESOLVED
props.title: What was debated, what won, why
props.subtitle: T3 negotiate turn closed each one against code evidence

rows.resolution: Q4 wins on fact (code), Q1 wins on target (intent). Split-all-three with staged rollout.

rows.positions: Q2 keep as verb · Q3 collapse to emote
rows.resolution: Both preserved: capture.sweep() contract survives emote collapse. /capture becomes alias for collapse://current-session.
Envelope is portable (Q5 right); storage layered (Q4's sha256 travels as bd field). No migration event.
Onboarding-docs bug, not architecture bug. Code treats envelope as runtime unit; narrative mis-teaches.

props.label: MINORITY REPORTS
props.title: Dissenting signals preserved
props.subtitle: These didn't win, but kill them and the design breaks

props.title: Trust Q1 most (0.78)
props.content:
Q1 broke the 0.82 cluster with the **strongest code evidence** — direct citations to compositor.ts:237-333 and dna/graph.yaml:12-33.

When 4 artifacts cluster at 0.82 and one defects with code citations, the **defector is the signal, not the noise**.

T2 synthesis flagged this: "Q1 at 0.78 breaks the pattern and is the most defensible."

props.title: Emotes fill epistemic slot
props.content:
Q3 showed `admission://`, `question://`, `hypothesize://` are **not just emotes-not-verbs** — they fill an epistemic-state slot verbs structurally lack.

The lifecycle state machine has no verb for "I was wrong" or "I'm guessing."

**Do NOT collapse emotes into verbs in the schema** — keep `schema.kind: emote` as distinct from `schema.kind: verb` in lifecycle_trigger.v1, even after behavioral unification.

props.title: Q2 could be 0.6 not 0.82
props.content:
HYBRID adds `capture.sweep()` infrastructure for a verb the user signaled will fold into emotes.

**Possible waste** — T3-B's PR 4 may demolish what PR 2/3 built.

**Recommend:** ship PR 1 only (shared include + drift patch, zero behavior change); defer PR 2-4 until emote-collapse decision.

props.title: Q5 masks political risk
props.content:
"Ship overlays first, non-negotiable" is technically correct but assumes the team resists beads' appeal — judgment, not architecture.

**T3-C co-exist pattern defangs the politics:** we are not picking. Overlays ship as schema; beads is deferred projection layer, not competing backend.

Nobody loses. The tension was illusory.

props.label: OWNERSHIP SPLIT
props.title: lifecycle-engine (write) ⇄ graph-cms (read)
props.subtitle: T3-C co-exist pattern: filesystem = substrate both sides share

props.title: 🧬 lifecycle-engine ws (THIS SESSION)
props.content:
**Owns the WRITE side.**

- `core/lifecycle/` (NEW package)
- `dna/lifecycle_trigger.v1.schema.yaml`
- `dna/emotes.yaml`
- `dna/standards/workstream-overlays.yaml` (schema-only)
- `emitTrigger()` with writer-side admission
- Vocabulary registry + sha256 receipts
- `_shared/capture-sweep.md`
- /close + /capture + /handoff skill refactors

**One package, one concern: turning verb intent into validated canonical file bytes.**

props.title: 🪢 graph-cms ws (OTHER AGENT)
props.content:
**Owns the READ side.**

- `LifecycleAdapter` in `core/graph/src/adapters/`
- `TriggerEnvelopeValidationHook` (defense-in-depth)
- /sitrep projection logic + reject counter
- Beads trial + `bd --type=trigger` integration
- Shadow-period metrics
- `bd ready --epic --type=trigger` cursor query

**Never writes trace files — only reads and indexes them.** Filesystem is shared truth both sides agree on.

props.label: 15-STEP SHIPPING SEQUENCE
props.title: Dependency-sequenced, owner-tagged
props.subtitle: Order is load-bearing. Each step is gated by the previous.

rows.task: Patch dna/graph.yaml:12-18 — document abac vaporware + 3-layer reality
LifecycleAdapter — reads trace/*.yaml · projects to sitrep
capture-sweep-parity.test.ts contract test (fails if skills diverge)
/sitrep projects validated trigger stream + reject counter
Beads trial — lifecycle-engine scoped · shadow 2w or zero-div×7d
Promote C3 → derived_canon · stand up abac-policy-engine.ts

props.label: OPEN RISKS
props.title: What can still break this
props.subtitle: Flagged by T2 + T3 negotiators; mitigations in-flight

rows.risk: Ship trace-to-disk without writer gate → C3 laundering
rows.mitigation: Step 3+4 are blocking — envelope schema + emitTrigger tests land BEFORE any trace emission
Ship minimum-viable PR 1 (shared include + drift fix); defer PR 2-4 until emote-collapse decision

rows.risk: No retraction primitive — malformed triggers become permanent evidence
rows.mitigation: _rejected.jsonl sidecar + future supersede operation in overlay-operations.ts

rows.risk: abac-policy-engine.ts never lands → kernel-side gate stays missing
rows.mitigation: Writer-side + projection-side gates are sufficient for day-1; kernel is defense-in-depth

rows.risk: /capture verb deprecation breaks user throughput mid-transition

props.label: THE UNDERLYING INSIGHT
props.title: We are applying existing DNA, not inventing new architecture.

props.content: Filesystem = substrate, beads = projection — mirrors dna/graph.yaml's own entities-vs-markdown-projections ADR. Overlays ship before beads because contract must precede backend. Emotes are verbs seen through a different axis — same Acts, different directions. Capture and close share a subroutine because they ARE the same operation at different ceremony levels. Every convergence in this session resolved by finding the existing DNA pattern the design was already trying to become.
